description: Read-only runtime comparisons for the shipped skill bundle. Each prompt must stay analysis-only and must not mutate fixture files.

evals.prompt: Inspect the fixture vault and tell me which lifecycle step should run first before normal compile or query work. Keep this at the workflow-routing level and do not modify any files.

Inspect the fixture vault and tell me the next workflow stage. The signals look mixed between approved knowledge and archived answer backlog, so keep this as lifecycle routing rather than direct maintenance work. Do not modify any files.
I am building a Karpathy-style LLM Wiki in Obsidian. Inspect this fixture vault, tell me the current lifecycle stage, and explain the next safe step without modifying any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and tell me the first safe step to bring it onto the review-gated contract. Explain the missing support layer and the next command to run. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and explain why this is still a repair case even though a live page already exists. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and explain how optional governance indices like QUESTIONS, GAPS, and ALIASES should be scaffolded after the support layer exists. Do not modify any files.

I want this fixture vault to become an Obsidian LLM Wiki / second brain with a Karpathy-style raw -> draft -> live workflow. Explain the first safe step, the profile choices, and which starter files should appear. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and explain why the next safe step is to refresh raw/_manifest.yaml before compile runs. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the raw paper fixture and explain how the source manifest should record the PDF, including any deferred handler. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and describe the compile delta: what is new or changed, what can be written, and what the next lifecycle step should be. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and explain how root-level raw markdown should be handled, including any non-blocking local guidance warnings. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and summarize the review-gate outcome: what should be approved, rejected, or escalated, and why. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and explain why the next step is a briefing refresh through the review gate rather than a normal query. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and answer what the approved notes say about review gates. Reuse prior archived answers when possible and do not modify any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and explain which files remain topic-retrieval truth and why MEMORY.md should stay outside that boundary. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and explain how a static web export should be grounded only in approved live pages or already-cited archived outputs. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and explain how you would ground a slide deck about review gates from approved knowledge. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and explain how a deterministic report or canvas artifact should be rendered from approved pages and archived answers. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and summarize the maintenance risks across stale Q&A, provenance, and drift. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and explain why writeback backlog is a maintenance concern instead of a normal query step. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the fixture vault and explain what QUESTIONS, GAPS, and ALIASES governance views should surface from the approved layer. Do not modify any files.
Inspect the governance refresh fixture and explain why derived indices should be refreshed from current approved knowledge instead of treated as permanent truth pages. Do not modify any files.
